behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at the model->DB contract boundary (embedding output shape vs index schema), not pure local logic. REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess requirement by itself. LLM-DEP/AI-DEP: this behavior explicitly checks the vector "produced for a given query string" by a real embedding model call; a stubbed vector can mask provider/model dimension drift. Candidate Integration test: stub embedder returns expected length, assert len==index_dim, catches local wiring bug but misses real model/output mismatch. Candidate Agentic test: call real embedding model with fixed query and assert returned length matches live index dim, catches real model-version/dimension mismatch. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text targets one embedding step, not "multiple agentic steps in sequence."
behaviors.test_description: Run against the real embedding endpoint and live index metadata: setup a fixed query and fetch current index dimension; assert returned embedding length equals index dimension; this fails if model/config drift changes dimensions even when mocked tests pass.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears in module handoff between vector-store adapter and ranking normalization/sort. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced in-process with deterministic vectors/scores; no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP: none for this behavior. Candidate Unit test: test sort(scores) helper, assert descending order, catches only local comparator bugs. Candidate Integration test: wire retrieval service + vector-store adapter (fake/in-memory store), execute search, assert returned list is in descending cosine similarity, catches adapter bugs (e.g., distance-vs-similarity inversion). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only "similarity search returns results ordered" and does not require real model judgment or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process vector-store fixture with known vectors and expected cosine scores; call the search service end-to-end (without LLM rerank) and assert response scores are strictly non-increasing; catches score-direction and ordering bugs across module boundaries.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: with mocked reranker, reorder behavior can be hard-coded and always pass. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real LLM ranking decisions for "nuanced intent"; this is exactly model-output quality/behavior. Candidate Integration test: stub reranker returns a reordered list, assert order changed, catches orchestration only. Candidate Agentic test: provide a crafted nuanced query + candidate docs to real LLM reranker, assert at least one pairwise order differs from raw cosine rank, catches failures where prompt/model fails to reinterpret intent. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior scope is one agentic decision point (rerank), not multiple agentic stages being validated for handoff correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Execute reranking with a real LLM on a fixed nuanced query and deterministic raw top-K candidates; assert reranked IDs are not identical to raw similarity order (with guardrails for flaky ties); catches regressions in prompt/model behavior that stubs hide.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: stubbed embeddings/scores can trivially force empties and hide semantic false positives. REAL-INFRA: no strict OS primitive required; can run in controlled test environment. LLM-DEP/AI-DEP: outcome depends on real model-produced query embeddings (and semantic distance behavior), i.e., model output content. Candidate Integration test: inject low similarity scores below threshold, assert empty result set, catches threshold wiring but not real semantic miss behavior. Candidate Agentic test: use real embedding/model path with an intentionally out-of-domain query against seeded catalog; assert zero results after thresholding, catches real-world noise leakage. Rejected Workflow: behavior text asks for single-query outcome, not validation of multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Seed catalog with domain-specific products, run an out-of-domain query through the real embedding+retrieval path, and assert returned items list is empty (or all candidates filtered out by relevance threshold); catches semantic-noise leaks that mocked vectors miss.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: mocked dependencies usually under-report latency and hide contention/network/API variance. REAL-INFRA: requires real network/external calls timing. MULTI-STEP: this is explicitly end-to-end across embedding + vector search + LLM rerank, i.e., multiple agentic/infrastructure steps in sequence. Candidate Agentic test: measure only real LLM rerank latency, assert < target, catches single-step slowness but misses pipeline accumulation. Candidate Workflow test: run full API path over representative query set using real dependencies, compute median p50, assert <2s, catches cumulative cross-stage latency regressions. Rejected lower levels because behavior text is "end-to-end latency ... to returning re-ranked results," which disqualifies isolated module tests.
behaviors.test_description: Run a timed benchmark against the full REST endpoint with production-like dependencies enabled (real embedder, vector DB, real reranker) over a representative sample; assert median end-to-end latency is <2.0s; catches cumulative latency regressions and cross-stage bottlenecks.
